Title: Stopping Distance — Dry Road
Statement: The brake system shall bring the vehicle to a complete stop from 100 km/h on a dry asphalt surface (peak friction coefficient μ ≥ 0.9) within 36 metres, measured from the point of initial brake pedal contact, in compliance with FMVSS 135.

Title: Stopping Distance — Wet Road
Statement: The brake system shall bring the vehicle to a complete stop from 80 km/h on a wet asphalt surface (peak friction coefficient μ ≥ 0.45) within 55 metres, measured from the point of initial brake pedal contact.

Title: ISO 26262 ASIL-D for ABS ECU
Statement: The Anti-lock Braking System Electronic Control Unit shall be developed and verified to Automotive Safety Integrity Level D (ASIL-D) in accordance with ISO 26262-5 for hardware and ISO 26262-6 for software.

Title: Brake Fade Resistance
Statement: After 10 successive full-stop brake applications from 100 km/h at 30-second intervals, the brake system stopping distance shall not increase by more than 20% compared to the first application.

Title: Brake System Availability
Statement: The primary hydraulic brake circuit shall maintain braking capability following a single-point failure in any one hydraulic circuit, providing at least 50% of nominal deceleration force on the remaining circuit.

Title: Pedal Feel and Travel
Statement: Under normal braking conditions, the brake pedal shall not travel more than 115 mm from the at-rest position to achieve full system pressure. Pedal effort at maximum deceleration shall not exceed 500 N.

Title: Warning Lamp Activation
Statement: The brake system shall illuminate the brake warning lamp on the instrument cluster within 2 seconds of detecting any of the following: low brake fluid level, hydraulic pressure loss in either circuit, or loss of ABS/ESC ECU communication.

Title: Operating Temperature Range
Statement: All brake system components shall operate within specification across an ambient temperature range of -40°C to +85°C for electronics and -40°C to +400°C for brake disc and caliper assemblies.

Title: Dual-Circuit Hydraulic Layout
Statement: The hydraulic brake circuit shall be configured in a diagonal split (X-split) layout, with each circuit serving one front wheel and the diagonally opposite rear wheel, ensuring deceleration is maintained upon loss of either circuit.

Title: Master Cylinder Pressure
Statement: The brake master cylinder shall generate a maximum hydraulic line pressure of 170 bar under panic braking (pedal force ≥ 450 N), measured at the ABS modulator inlet port.

Title: Hydraulic Fluid Specification
Statement: The brake hydraulic system shall be designed for DOT 4 glycol-ether brake fluid with a dry boiling point of at least 230°C and a wet boiling point of at least 155°C.

Title: Vacuum Brake Booster
Statement: The vacuum brake booster shall provide a minimum boost ratio of 4:1 at an inlet vacuum of 600 mbar, and shall maintain at least two full-power brake applications following engine stall without engine vacuum assist.

Title: Brake Line Burst Pressure
Statement: All hydraulic brake lines and fittings shall withstand a hydrostatic burst pressure of not less than 700 bar without rupture or permanent deformation, as verified by component qualification testing.

Title: ABS Activation Threshold
Statement: The Anti-lock Braking System shall detect incipient wheel lock-up by monitoring individual wheel deceleration and shall activate pressure modulation within 15 ms of the wheel slip ratio exceeding 15%.

Title: ABS Wheel Speed Sensor Accuracy
Statement: The wheel speed sensors shall provide speed measurements accurate to within ±0.5 km/h across the vehicle speed range of 5 km/h to 250 km/h, at signal update rates of not less than 100 Hz.

Title: ABS Steering Retention
Statement: During ABS intervention, the system shall retain sufficient steering authority to allow the driver to navigate around obstacles while maintaining maximum achievable deceleration.

Title: ABS Deactivation Conditions
Statement: The ABS system shall automatically deactivate and revert to standard braking at vehicle speeds below 5 km/h to avoid excessive brake torque oscillation at near-stop conditions.

Title: ABS ECU Fault Detection
Statement: The ABS ECU shall perform a self-diagnostic cycle at every ignition-on event. Any detected fault in a wheel speed sensor, solenoid valve, or ECU internal memory shall be stored as a DTC and reported over CAN within 500 ms.

Title: Electronic Brake-Force Distribution
Statement: The EBD function shall dynamically apportion braking force between front and rear axles based on measured vehicle deceleration, maintaining rear wheel slip ratio below front wheel slip ratio under all loading conditions.

Title: EBD Load Adaptation
Statement: The EBD function shall adapt brake-force distribution in response to changes in vehicle laden mass from kerb weight to maximum permissible gross vehicle mass without requiring driver input.

Title: Electronic Stability Control Yaw Correction
Statement: The ESC system shall detect vehicle yaw rate deviation exceeding 5 deg/s from the driver-intended path and apply selective individual wheel braking within 20 ms to restore directional stability.

Title: ESC Traction Control Integration
Statement: The ESC controller shall share wheel speed sensor data, yaw rate sensor, and lateral accelerometer inputs with the Traction Control System via a shared CAN message. Data refresh cycle shall not exceed 10 ms.

Title: ESC UN Regulation R13-H Compliance
Statement: The Electronic Stability Control system shall comply with UNECE Regulation No. 13-H for passenger vehicles, as mandatory for all markets of vehicle sale.

Title: Electric Parking Brake Hold Force
Statement: The Electric Parking Brake shall generate a clamping force sufficient to hold the vehicle stationary on a 30% gradient (16.7°) at maximum permissible gross vehicle mass under both nose-up and nose-down orientations.

Title: Auto Park Apply
Statement: The EPB system shall automatically apply the parking brake when the vehicle ignition is switched off with the transmission in park or neutral, vehicle stationary, and driver door open, completing application within 1 second.

Title: Emergency Braking via EPB
Statement: When activated by the driver at vehicle speed above 10 km/h, the EPB shall apply a progressive ramp of rear axle braking force to achieve deceleration of not less than 3.0 m/s², without triggering ESC intervention.

Title: Wheel Speed Sensor Type
Statement: All four wheel speed sensors shall use active magnetoresistive technology providing a digital frequency output signal from 0 km/h (active standstill detection) with a minimum air gap tolerance of 0 to 2.5 mm.

Title: Yaw Rate and Lateral g Sensor
Statement: The combined yaw rate and lateral acceleration sensor shall measure yaw rate over ±100 deg/s and lateral acceleration over ±1.5 g with a resolution of 0.1 deg/s and 0.01 g respectively, at an output rate of 100 Hz.

Title: Brake Fluid Level Sensor
Statement: The master cylinder reservoir shall incorporate a fluid level sensor that triggers a warning flag on the CAN bus within 500 ms of the fluid level dropping below the minimum mark.

Title: Brake Wear Indicator
Statement: A contact-type brake pad wear indicator shall be fitted to the inner pad of each front axle. The indicator shall trigger a warning CAN signal when pad thickness reaches 2.5 mm (approximately 20% remaining life).

Title: CAN Bus Interface
Statement: The ABS/ESC ECU shall communicate with the Engine Management, Transmission Control, and Body Control Module ECUs over a dedicated chassis CAN bus operating at 500 kbit/s, conforming to ISO 11898-2.

Title: OBD-II Diagnostic Access
Statement: The brake system ECU shall expose all fault codes, live data parameters, and calibration functions via the OBD-II diagnostic port using ISO 15765-4 (CAN) transport protocol, compliant with SAE J1979.

Title: Brake Signal to ADAS
Statement: The ABS/ESC ECU shall transmit a 'Brakes Active' boolean flag and current estimated deceleration value to the ADAS Domain Controller over the chassis CAN bus at a cycle time of 10 ms.

Title: FMVSS 135 Certification
Statement: The complete brake system shall pass all tests defined in FMVSS 135 (Light Vehicle Brake Systems), including fade, recovery, spike, and cold effectiveness tests, before series production release.

Title: Corrosion Protection
Statement: All external brake system metallic components, including calipers, brackets, and hydraulic lines, shall withstand 240 hours of neutral salt spray testing (ISO 9227) without base metal corrosion that impairs function.

Title: Electromagnetic Compatibility
Statement: The ABS/ESC ECU and all associated wiring harnesses shall comply with ISO 11452 (component-level RF immunity) and CISPR 25 (radiated emissions) for automotive applications.

Title: Brake System Service Life
Statement: The brake master cylinder, ABS modulator, and ESC ECU shall have a designed service life of not less than 200,000 km or 15 years (whichever occurs first) under normal vehicle usage profiles.
